div.div: · Tower of Hanoi apparatus: 3 pegs and 8 discs of decreasing size

Stack all discs on the left peg in order, largest at the bottom. Move the entire stack to the right peg using the center peg as a helper. Rules: move only one disc at a time; never place a larger disc on a smaller one. The minimum moves for n discs is 2ⁿ - 1. For 3 discs: 7 moves. For 8 discs: 255 moves. The key strategy is recursive: move the top n-1 discs to the spare peg, move the largest disc to the target, then move the n-1 discs on top of it. Tower of Hanoi sets are widely sold and also available as free online simulators.

Édouard Lucas invented Tower of Hanoi in 1883 and marketed it with a legend: Brahmin monks in Benares are transferring 64 golden discs. When they finish, the world will end. At one move per second, completing 64 discs takes about 585 billion years — longer than the current age of the universe.

Eight discs of decreasing size are stacked on one peg, largest at the bottom. Moving only one disc at a time, and never placing a larger disc on a smaller one, transfer the entire stack to another peg. The minimum number of moves for eight discs is 255.

svg.text: Source: Puzzles Old and New (1893) · Heritage Parlor
